title: Close the `git commit <pathspec>` bypass of the H-09b/H-10b security gate
evidence: Line 313 `added = added_lines(cwd, "--cached")` scans only the staged diff; the worktree diff is added ONLY when `COMMIT_ALL_RE.search(commit.group("args")) or add` (line 314). `git commit -m x src/crypto.js` (a pathspec commit, no `-a`, no `git add` in the same command) commits the WORKTREE content of src/crypto.js, which `git diff --cached` does not show. Neither condition fires, so `sensitive` is empty and the crypto/secret gate passes with no recorded security-gate pass.
impact: A crypto/TLS or hardcoded-secret change can be committed past the load-bearing H-09b/H-10b blocking gate by naming the file as a commit pathspec instead of `git add`-ing it — exactly the spelling an untrusted model would reach for to evade the gate. Defeats the commit-time enforcement that security-controls.md treats as the load-bearing control.
recommendation: When COMMIT_RE matches and its args contain any non-flag pathspec token (after the `--` separator), union the worktree-vs-HEAD diff (`git diff HEAD -- <paths>`) into the scanned set, the same way `-a`/`add` already do. Simpler/safer: always include the worktree diff for the named pathspec, since a bare `git commit <path>` commits worktree content regardless of the index.
acceptance_criteria: A test commits an unstaged crypto/secret change via `git commit -m x <path>` (no -a, no prior git add) and pre-bash.py exits 2 with H-09b/H-10b
Existing H-09b allow/block cases still pass

title: Close the `git commit <pathspec>` bypass of the H-14 migration gate
evidence: Line 356 `staged = staged_paths(cwd)` lists only `git diff --cached --name-only`; line 357 unions `worktree_paths(cwd)` ONLY when `COMMIT_ALL_RE.search(commit.group("args")) or add`. `git commit -m x db/migrate/001.sql` commits the worktree migration without staging it, so it is absent from `staged_paths`, `migs` is empty, and the H-14 migration-review gate never fires.
impact: An unreviewed (and, since binding is by content digest, an edited/mutated) database migration can be committed past the H-14 backstop by naming it as a commit pathspec — the same evasion class as appsec-001, defeating migration immutability/review enforcement at commit time.
recommendation: Mirror the appsec-001 fix: when the commit names pathspec tokens, union the worktree paths (or `git diff HEAD --name-only -- <paths>`) into `staged` before the is_migration_path filter, so a pathspec-commit of a worktree migration is covered.
acceptance_criteria: A test commits an unstaged migration via `git commit -m x <migration-path>` and pre-bash.py exits 2 with H-14
Existing H-14 backstop tests still pass

title: H-05 audit-log append-only shell guard is bypassable via variable/indirect targets
evidence: LOG_DESTROY_RE/LOG_TRUNC_RE require the literal log name to appear adjacent to a destroy verb or `>` in the command string (anchored on `"overrides.log" in cmd` etc., line 289). A command that targets the log through an indirect name defeats both: e.g. PowerShell `$f='overrides.log'; rm $f` (the `;` separates the literal from `rm $f`, and `rm $f` carries no literal) or `f=.codearbiter/overrides.log; rm "$f"` in bash. The hook fires on PowerShell too, but the guard is purely lexical. The N-3 comment (lines 61-67) accepts fd-redirect residual risk but does not cover verb-with-variable targets.
impact: The append-only audit trail (overrides.log / triage.log / sprint-log.md) — a load-bearing, unrecoverable integrity control per security-controls.md §Audit trail — can be truncated or deleted by the untrusted model using a one-line variable indirection, with no /ca:override audit entry. Low severity only because it requires deliberate two-step indirection and the trail is reconstructable from history in many cases.
recommendation: Do not rely solely on lexical regex for an unrecoverable control. Add a path-aware backstop: a PostToolUse(Bash) check that detects the audit logs shrank/disappeared after the command (compare line count / inode), or a chattr/readonly + sanctioned-append-only writer; at minimum extend the residual-risk note to name variable-indirection so triage is informed.
acceptance_criteria: `$f=overrides.log; rm $f` (and the bash equivalent) either blocks or is detected post-hoc as an append-only violation
The accepted-residual-risk note explicitly enumerates variable/indirect-target spellings if a full fix is deferred
